content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • • Administration • • • Executive Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
(916) 322 5662        322 5660        322 5901        322 6441        322 6444

March 6, 1979

Kenneth Hahn, Chairman
Board of Supervisors
County of Los Angeles
866 Hall of Administration
Los Angeles, CA 90012

Dear Mr. Hahn:

Thank you for your letter of February 14, 1979 regarding
disclosure of complimentary passes. As you know, the Fair
Political Practices Commission specifically addressed the
question of disclosing complimentary passes and tickets in
an opinion issued to William Hopkins 3 FPPC Opinions 107
(No. 77-022, Dec. 8, 1977). In its deliberations prior to
issuance of the opinion, the Commission received extensive
testimony similar to the views contained in your letter.
Nevertheless, the Commission concluded that the Political
Reform Act required disclosure of such complimentary passes
based on the fair market value of such passes at the time they
were received. The Commission concluded that the Act did not
permit the value of such passes to be calculated in terms of
the number of times the pass was used by the official.

I appreciate the time and thought which went into your
letter. If the Political Reform Act were drafted differently,
the Commission might have reached the conclusion urged in your
letter.

If I may be of further assistance, please do not hesitate
to contact me.

Sincerely,

Ted Prim
Ted Prim, Chief
Conflicts of Interest Division

TP:nrw

79042

666 HALL OF ADMINISTRATION
974-2222

MEMBERS OF THE BOARD

PETER F SCHABARUM
KENNETH HAHN
EDMUND D EDELMAN
JAMES A HAYES
BAXTER WARD

County of Los Angeles
Board of Supervisors
Los Angeles,California 90012

KENNETH HAHN
CHAIRMAN
BOARD OF SUPERVISORS

February 14, 1979

Mr. Daniel H. Lowenstein, Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Mr. Lowenstein:

I have been informed by our County Counsel that in filing
the annual Statement of Economic Disclosure, certain values
must be given to complimentary passes, whether or not they
have been used. For example, an annual Disneyland pass has
been valued by the Fair Political Practices Commission at
$620.

Before Disneyland was constructed, the late Walt Disney
told me he was going to send me a pass. I attended the
opening of Disneyland, and each year since, I have been sent
a complimentary pass. Last year, I believe I used the pass
once, and perhaps twice the year before. I have received a
pass this year and I might not even go to the park.

Wouldn't it be more fair and accurate to list the times I
used the pass and value it accordingly? That would provide
the real value as far as my use is concerned. The same
practice would apply to reporting other complimentary passes
which are sent to me unsolicited. It seems to me this prac-
tice would achieve truth-in-reporting in a simple and logical
way.

Sincerely yours,

KENNETH HAHN
Chairman

KH:mw

sections.question: Wouldn't it be more fair and accurate to list the times I used the pass and value it accordingly? That would provide the real value as far as my use is concerned. The same practice would apply to reporting other complimentary passes which are sent to me unsolicited.
sections.question_synthetic: Should complimentary passes be valued based on actual usage rather than their full market value when filing annual Statements of Economic Disclosure?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No conclusion or advice is provided in the document. The question is clear but appears in the body of the letter rather than in a formal section header.

embedding.summary: Kenneth Hahn, Chairman of the Los Angeles County Board of Supervisors, inquires whether complimentary passes (such as an annual Disneyland pass valued at $620) should be reported based on actual usage rather than full market value on his Statement of Economic Disclosure.
